description: A photograph depicting the Glacier des Bossons in the French Alps. The image shows a vast expanse of snow and ice covering rugged mountain peaks under a clear blue sky. Rocky formations are visible in the foreground, partially covered by snow, indicating a permanent snowfield environment. The image is credited to K. Larsson and illustrates the Copernicus Land Monitoring Service (CLMS) CORINE Land Cover (CLC) class 6.3.3.0, which is defined as "Glaciers and Perpetual Snow."
